meta.title: Bahasa Indonesia Affixes
meta.description: Multiple-choice deck testing the grammatical function of common Indonesian prefixes, suffixes, and circumfixes using real example words.

cards.question: Prefix 'me-' in 'menulis' (from 'tulis', meaning 'write') indicates which grammatical function?
choices: Passive voice - the action is done to the subject
Active voice - the subject performs the action
A noun referring to a person
cards.explanation: The me- prefix (meN-) forms active transitive verbs, marking the subject as the one performing the action: 'menulis' = 'to write'.

cards.question: Prefix 'di-' in 'ditulis' (from 'tulis', meaning 'write') indicates what?
choices: Active voice, subject performs the action
Passive voice - the action is done to the subject
cards.explanation: The di- prefix forms the passive voice: 'ditulis' means 'is written' or 'was written (by someone)'.

cards.question: Prefix 'ber-' in 'bekerja' (from 'kerja', meaning 'work') functions to...
choices: form a passive verb meaning 'to be worked'
form an intransitive verb meaning 'to work / be engaged in work'
form a causative verb meaning 'to make someone work'
cards.explanation: Ber- typically forms intransitive verbs expressing a state or activity the subject is engaged in, without a direct object: 'bekerja' = 'to work'.

cards.question: Prefix 'ter-' in 'terbuka' (from 'buka', meaning 'open') most often conveys...
choices: an unintentional/accidental result or resulting state - 'accidentally opened / already open'
cards.explanation: Ter- frequently marks an unintended action or a resultant state, distinguishing it from the deliberate active form 'membuka' (to open something on purpose).

cards.question: Prefix 'per-' in 'perbaiki' (from 'baik', meaning 'good') functions as...
choices: a causative marker meaning 'to make good / fix, improve'
a passive marker meaning 'to be made good by someone'
an agent noun marker meaning 'the good one'
cards.explanation: Per- (often combined with -i as perbaiki) is causative, turning the adjective 'baik' (good) into a verb meaning 'to fix/improve'.

cards.question: Suffix '-kan' in 'siapkan' (from 'siap', meaning 'ready') adds what meaning?
cards.explanation: -kan is a causative/benefactive suffix, turning 'siap' (ready) into 'siapkan' meaning 'prepare something (for someone)'.

cards.question: Suffix '-i' in 'mengunjungi' (from 'kunjung', meaning 'visit') typically indicates...
choices: a locative/repetitive action directed at an object - 'to visit (a place/person, thoroughly or repeatedly)'
cards.explanation: The -i suffix often marks a locative or iterative sense, indicating the action is directed thoroughly or repeatedly at its object, as in 'mengunjungi' (to visit).

cards.question: Circumfix 'ke-...-an' in 'kesehatan' (from 'sehat', meaning 'healthy') forms...
cards.explanation: Ke-...-an turns an adjective into an abstract noun describing a state or quality: 'sehat' (healthy) becomes 'kesehatan' (health).

cards.question: Circumfix 'pe-...-an' in 'pendidikan' (from 'didik', meaning 'educate') forms...
choices: a noun describing the process/result of educating - 'education'
a passive verb meaning 'to be educated'
cards.explanation: Pe-...-an forms nouns describing a process or its result: 'mendidik' (to educate) becomes 'pendidikan' (education).

cards.question: Prefix 'se-' in 'serumah' (from 'rumah', meaning 'house') conveys the meaning...
choices: the same/one house, i.e., living in the same household
cards.explanation: Se- means 'one' or 'the same', so 'serumah' means sharing the same house/household.

cards.question: Prefix 'memper-' in 'mempercepat' (from 'cepat', meaning 'fast') indicates...
choices: a causative verb meaning 'to speed up / make faster'
a passive verb meaning 'to be sped up by someone'
cards.explanation: Memper- is a causative prefix combining meN- and per-, turning the adjective 'cepat' (fast) into a verb meaning 'to make faster/accelerate'.

cards.question: Prefix 'pe-' in 'penulis' (from 'tulis', meaning 'write') forms...
choices: an agent noun meaning 'writer' - the person who does the action
an abstract noun meaning 'the act of writing'
a passive verb meaning 'to be written'
cards.explanation: Pe- forms agent nouns denoting the doer of an action: 'penulis' = 'writer', the person who writes.

cards.question: Suffix '-an' in 'makanan' (from 'makan', meaning 'eat') forms...
choices: a noun meaning 'food' - the result/object of the verb
cards.explanation: -an is a nominalizing suffix that often names the object or result of a verb: 'makan' (eat) becomes 'makanan' (food).

cards.question: Circumfix 'per-...-an' in 'perusahaan' (from 'usaha', meaning 'effort/business') forms...
cards.explanation: Per-...-an forms abstract or institutional nouns: 'usaha' (effort/business) becomes 'perusahaan' (company/enterprise).

cards.question: Prefix 'ter-' in 'terbaik' (from 'baik', meaning 'good') here functions as a marker of...
cards.explanation: Beyond its accidental/resultative use, ter- also forms superlatives with adjectives: 'baik' (good) becomes 'terbaik' (the best).
